LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MARCIA BROWNE SPECIAL ANTI-SLAPP MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: 1. CCP SECTION 425.16(F) REQUIRES THAT AN ANTI-SLAPP MOTION BE FILED WITHIN SIXTY DAYS OF SERVICE. HERE, SERVICE WAS MADE ON OCTOBER 28, 2008. 2. MOTION IS DENIED. DEFENDANT'S MOTION IS UNTIMELY UNDER CCP SECTION 425.16(F) AND DEFENDANT HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED GOOD CAUSE FOR RELIEF. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR AWARD SANCTIONS ARE DENIED.(PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: QUENTIN KOPP; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR 5797
MINI-MINUTES FOR AUG-24-2009 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-24-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MARCIA BROWNE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for Summary Judgment is Granted. No opposition filed. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Ntc And Mtn And Special Anti-Slapp Mtn To Strike Pltfs Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-14-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MARCIA BROWNE Special Anti-SLAPP Motion To Strike Plaintiff's Complaint. Appearances: Antonio Molinar, Esq. Plaintiff in Pro Per and opposing party. There are no other appearances. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: 1. CCP section 425.16(f) requires that an anti-SLAPP motion be filed within sixty days of service. Here, service was made on October 28, 2008. 2. Motion is Denied. Defendant's motion is untimely under CCP section 425.16(f) and defendant has not demonstrated good cause for relief. The Court adds: Plaintiff's request for Award Sanctions are denied.(Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302KOPP/PB)Judge: Quentin Kopp; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5797

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-02-2009 Set For Trial On Sep-28-09 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Sep-10-09 At 03:30 P.M. In Dept 303

Notice And Motion To Disqualify Deft Counsel
Off Calendar Feb-06-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF ANTONIO MOLINAR'S Motion To Disqualify Defendant's Counsel, James Bruen, Esq. Appearances: Antonio Molinar, Plaintiff, in Pro Per, appearing, and moving party; There are no other appearances. (Plaintiff in Pro Per failed to give the opposing party notice as required in the local rules, that he planned to attend the hearing.) The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion is denied. Failure to maintain evidentiary burden. Defendant's request for sanctions is denied because CCP section 128.5 is inapplicable to this action. See CCP section 128.5(b)(1).Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/CWW/PB) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5797
